content: Comparing the intrinsic reward mechanisms of curiosity-driven RL methods (ICM vs RND vs hand-designed curious agents) reveals that an agent's own intrinsic valuation of novel memory is the unifying bottleneck for both performance on long-horizon sparse reward tasks and active learning state-of-the-art gains. Hierarchically modifying an agent's intrinsic curiosity signal to weight unexpected episodic memory activations, rather than just state prediction error, can create more sample-efficient curious agents that outperform existing 2024 SOTA active learning agents on open-ended exploration tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
